cit.basis: Standard 16% corporate income tax (pelno mokestis) from 1 January 2025, raised from 15% by the 2025 tax reform.

small_company_criteria.note: Both conditions must be met: no more than 10 employees AND annual income not exceeding EUR 300,000. Rate raised from 5% to 6% on 1 January 2025.

cit.first_year_note: Qualifying small companies pay 0% in their first tax year.
cit.pillar_two_note: Lithuania applies the EU Pillar Two (Directive 2022/2523) 15% global minimum tax for groups with consolidated revenue >= EUR 750m — out of scope for this SME estimator.

vat.note: EU member state; One-Stop-Shop (OSS/IOSS) available for cross-border B2C supplies.

official_sources.name: State Tax Inspectorate (VMI, Valstybine mokesciu inspekcija)

effective_date_range.basis: Target ruleset/params year; jurisdiction fiscal calendars and transitional periods may differ.

rate_threshold_assumptions: Rates and thresholds are simplified for LedgerField estimates.
Small-company eligibility (related-party aggregation rules), incentives, participation exemption, and sector-specific rules may alter outcomes.
Verify against official guidance and local advisor before filing or production use.
limitations: Not legal, tax, accounting, or filing advice.
Does not encode every deduction, exemption, treaty, WHT, payroll, or filing rule.
High-stakes use requires local validation against official forms and current law.
